title: HHS Select Agents and Toxins
text: Federal Select Agent Program (HHS/CDC + USDA APHIS). Lists ~70 biological agents/toxins (e.g., Bacillus anthracis, Ebola, Yersinia pestis, ricin) that pose severe threat to public/animal/plant health. Entity registration via APHIS/CDC Form 1; inventory + access on Form 4. Tier 1 subset (15 agents) carries enhanced security. Responsible Official (RO) appointed at entity level.

title: Security Risk Assessment (FBI CJIS)
text: Each individual with access to select agents must hold an active FBI CJIS Security Risk Assessment (SRA). 5-year cycle. Excludes: minors, convicted of certain federal crimes, mental adjudication, fugitives, illegal aliens, dishonorably discharged, individuals from designated state sponsors of terrorism. Restricted persons (per 18 USC §175b) cannot be granted access.

title: Form 3 incident reporting
text: Theft, loss, or release of select agent or toxin must be reported to CDC DSAT (Division of Select Agents and Toxins) within 7 calendar days using APHIS/CDC Form 3. For releases that may pose immediate hazard: notify by telephone immediately + Form 3 within 7 days. Failure to report = enforcement action + possible suspension/revocation.

title: Tier 1 agents — enhanced security
text: Tier 1 select agents present greatest risk of deliberate misuse with most significant potential for mass casualties or devastating economic impact. Enhanced requirements: 3-tier access barriers, dual-key, intrusion detection, surveillance, suitability assessment, immediate notification of loss/theft. Includes: B. anthracis, Y. pestis, Ebola, smallpox, Marburg, F. tularensis, B. mallei + others.

title: Biosafety in Microbiological and Biomedical Laboratories
text: Authoritative US biosafety guidance. BSL-1/2/3/4 + ABSL-1/2/3/4 (animal). Risk assessment methodology. Primary barriers (BSC, centrifuge cups, PPE) + secondary barriers (facility design, ventilation, autoclave). Agent summary statements per agent. Risk Group 1-4 mapping to BSL containment.

title: Risk-based approach to laboratory biosafety
text: Replaces strict BSL designation with continuous risk assessment producing tailored mitigation. Core requirements (good microbiological practice + procedure), heightened control measures (BSC + dedicated room), maximum containment (full BSL-4). Emphasizes competency-based training + continuous improvement.

title: BSC certification
text: Class II biosafety cabinets certified annually per NSF/ANSI 49: HEPA filter integrity (DOP/PAO test, ≤0.01% penetration), downflow velocity (typ. 50 fpm), inflow velocity (≥100 fpm), airflow smoke pattern, alarm + interlocks, cabinet integrity. Cert by accredited field technician (NSF accreditation).

title: Dual-Use Research of Concern
text: DURC = life sciences research that could be misused to harm public health, agriculture, or national security. Institutional review for experiments with 15 listed agents + 7 categories (e.g., enhance pathogenicity, evade detection, enable evasion of vaccines, alter host range). Institutional Review Entity (IRE) + Risk Mitigation Plan.

title: Potential Pandemic Pathogens Care + Oversight
text: Federal-funded research that may create enhanced potential pandemic pathogens reviewed by HHS P3CO Review Group prior to funding. Criteria: likely to enhance transmissibility/virulence, likely to enable evasion of countermeasures. Goal: balance scientific value vs. risks of accidental release / misuse.

title: APHIS/CDC Form 2 transfer
text: Intra- + interstate transfer of select agents/toxins requires both sender + recipient to submit Form 2; APHIS/CDC must authorize transfer in writing before shipment. Packaging per IATA/DOT Category A. Receipt confirmation back to APHIS/CDC. Transfer without Form 2 = enforcement action.
